# Site Reliability Engineering Manager - Software Engineering - Mainframe job at FIS - Fidelity National Information Services in Jacksonville, FL, Milwaukee, WI
[...]
Title: Site Reliability Engineering Manager (Software Engineering - Mainframe)
[...]
We are FIS. Our technology powers the world’s economy and our teams bring innovation to life. We champion diversity to deliver the best products and solutions for our colleagues, clients and communities. If you’re ready to start learning, growing and making an impact with a career in fintech, we’d like to know: Are you FIS?
[...]
We are seeking a highly qualified Senior DevOps Manager to lead a larger practice of CI/CD operations across multiple application portfolio teams. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise of modern SDLC tooling, CI/CD best practices, and a proven track record of strong leadership.
[...]
This individual will play a key role in driving standardization, centralization, and modernization of existing Release Management workflow and architecture. Ideal candidate will successfully transition existing operations from Legacy tooling and execution practices towards greater observability, automation, and efficiencies. This is an opportunity to lead a team that will work across both mainframe technologies (COBOL, RPG) and modern server-based environments (Java, Angular, .NET), giving you a unique opportunity to operate at the intersection of legacy systems and contemporary microservices. This is a great opportunity to drive engineering improvements that directly enhance SDLC practices and production support operations. This role is with our Horizon team.
[...]
Management Priorities: Oversee CI/CD pipelines for a mainframe (Cobol, RPG, CL, JCL) dominated DevOps practice with a growing number of server-based (Java, .NET) DevOps initiatives.
[...]
Deliver Integrated mainframe and server development workflows with modern DevOps toolchains (e.g., Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, etc.).
[...]
Oversee multiple source control and release management workflows across existing development landscape including but not limited to Rocket Aldon, Bitbucket, Jenkins, GitHub, CXOne, Checkmarx, Artifactory, ServiceNow, and proprietary scripting.
[...]
Manage the design and implementation of an modernized automated end to end release management workflow.
[...]
Optimization & Governance: Drive continuous improvement, establish standards, implement monitoring/alerting (Splunk, Dynatrace), and manage security & compliance.
[...]
10+ years of experience in DevOps engineering, including CI/CD pipeline development and automation.
[...]
Experienced identifying and implementation automation and integration scripting using Jenkins, GitHub suite, Bitbucket, NUnit, Watir, Checkmarx, ServiceNow, Jira, and Azure DevOps Technologies and services.
[...]
Experience integrating modern version control (e.g., Git) with mainframe SCM tools (IBM Endevor, ISPW, Rational Developer for i/z, etc.).
[...]
Experience with IBM UrbanCode Deploy, z/OSMF, or Rocket Aldon.
[...]
Experience implementing automated testing on APM, Mainframe, and Server platforms.
[...]
Automation & Tooling: Develop automation scripts (Python, Bash, PowerShell), use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible), and manage containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
[...]
Experience in identifying and implementation automation and integration scripting using Jenkins, GitHub suite, Bitbucket, NUnit, Watir, Checkmarx, ServiceNow, Jira, and Azure DevOps Technologies and services.
[...]
Preferred Tooling Experience: Jenkins, GitLab, GitHub, Splunk, Dynatrace, Bit bucket, Jira.
[...]
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
[...]

results.title: Fraud Prevention Analyst I | Hirevector
highlights:
Fraud Prevention Analyst I | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Fraud Prevention Analyst I

Posted Jun 5, 2026

### About the position

- Are you curious, motivated, and forward-thinking? At FIS you’ll have the opportunity to work on some of the most challenging and relevant issues in financial services and technology. Our talented people empower us, and we believe in being part of a team that is open, collaborative, entrepreneurial, passionate and above all fun. Are you FIS?
- Please note: this is a full-time role with a hybrid schedule in the location posted.
- Current and/or future sponsorships are not available for this position.

- Responsibilities
- Analyze overall fraud metrics, identify, and report on emerging trends
- Test/Implement/Modify fraud strategies as appropriate
- Analyze fraud databases and report on operational performance
- Join fraud data with other data sources to derive end-to-end performance metrics for fraud
- Will work directly with clients, supporting fraud products and overall fraud needs
- Design and implement new internal and external Fraud reports
- Create and deliver presentations to clients with meaningful commentary on both past performance and future opportunities
- Collaborate with fraud management on special projects or requests
- Take ownership of work and proactively engage in identifying opportunities for improvement
- Respond to ad hoc requests for report building and data analysis
- Working independently, as well as with team members

- Requirements
- Advanced working with large-scale business data sets, fluent in scripting and rapid prototyping skills including expertise in SQL and Excel (3-5+ years of experience preferred)
- Possess a strong understanding of internal business segment (stakeholders) and presentation skills
- Experience in relational database structures, research methods, sampling techniques and system testing
- Are a go-getter, are self-directed and able to work independently
- Advanced data mining experience
- Familiar with BI tools (Hyperion, SAS, Python, Tableau, etc)
- Analytical mindset and love to crunch data
- Excellent communication skills both verbally and written

- Nice-to-haves
- Fraud background preferred (data driven/data analytics)
- Bilingual in Spanish

- Benefits
- Opportunities to innovate in fintech
- Tools for personal and professional growth
- Inclusive and diverse work environment
- Resources to invest in your community
- Competitive salary and benefits

results.title: AI Software Engineer – Fellow I at Fiserv | Remote Job | Kickstart Remote
highlights:
AI Software Engineer – Fellow I at Fiserv | Remote Job | Kickstart Remote
[...]
# AI Software Engineer – Fellow I
[...]
### Job Description
[...]
Engineering lead building a multi-tenant agentic AI platform for financial services with a focus on security and technical standards. Leading a high-performing team to drive platform success.
[...]
#### Responsibilities:
[...]
- Own end-to-end engineering delivery of the agentic AI platform
[...]
- Set technical vision and architecture direction for a multi-tenant agentic platform built on AWS Bedrock AgentCore, Strands Agents SDK, and Cedar/AVP authorization
[...]
- Recruit, hire, and develop pillar leads and senior engineers across Identity & Access, Agent Runtime, Observability & FinOps, Catalog & Registry, and Evals & Testing
[...]
- Drive platform security posture — threat modeling for agent delegation chains, prompt injection defence, cross-tenant isolation, and regulatory compliance (SOC2, OCC, FFIEC)
[...]
- Establish engineering standards — ADRs, SDK architecture patterns, code review culture
[...]
- Build and operate the agent onboarding pipeline — enabling adjacent business teams to build, certify, and deploy agents on the platform
[...]
- Define SLOs, operational readiness criteria, and incident response processes for a banking-grade production platform
[...]
- Manage cross-pillar dependencies and technical risk — credential flows, MCP Gateway governance, audit pipeline completeness, and multi-tenant data isolation
[...]
- Partner with Security to establish pre-GA sign-off gates including penetration testing, SAST/DAST integration, and compliance certification
[...]
- Drive FinOps discipline — per-tenant cost attribution, LLM usage optimization, and infrastructure right-sizing
[...]
#### Requirements:
[...]
- 10+ years of software engineering experience
- 5+ years leading engineering teams across multiple product areas or platform pillars
- Proven track record building and shipping multi-tenant SaaS platforms — ideally in financial services, payments, or regulated industries
[...]
- Deep hands-on experience with AWS (IAM, KMS, Cognito, DynamoDB, S3, Lambda, Step Functions, ECS/EKS) and infrastructure-as-code (CDK or Terraform)
[...]
- Strong background in platform engineering — SDK design, API governance, developer experience, and internal platform adoption
[...]
- Experience with identity and authorization architectures — federation, RBAC/ABAC, credential management, tenant isolation patterns
[...]
- Demonstrated ability to recruit, develop, and retain senior engineering talent in a high-growth environment
[...]
- Experience managing AWS or cloud vendor partnerships with embedded engineering resources
[...]
- Familiarity with security and compliance in financial services — threat modeling, penetration testing, audit requirements, data residency
[...]
- Strong architectural judgment — ability to make build-vs-buy decisions, set technical direction across distributed teams, and manage technical debt
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience).
[...]

results.title: Cloud Engineer (Kubernetes, AWS, Terraform) — FIS Global
highlights:
Senior Cloud Engineer for FIS Global's fintech platform — design and operate production Kubernetes (EKS) on AWS with Terraform, Ansible, Python and Bash, applying SRE practices at scale. 5-10 years, hybrid across Hyderabad / Bengaluru / Pune, INR 25-45 LPA band.
[...]
About this role
[...]
the team at FIS Global
[...]
Senior Cloud Engineer for FIS Global's fintech platform — design and operate production Kubernetes (EKS) on AWS with Terraform, Ansible, Python and Bash, applying SRE practices at scale. 5-10 years, hybrid across Hyderabad / Bengaluru / Pune, INR 25-45 LPA band. This listing on
[...]
Design, build and manage production Kubernetes clusters on Amazon EKS using Terraform for infrastructure-as-code, and create reusable Terraform modules for networking, security, storage and observability that other FIS engineering teams consume. Implement GitOps and CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins and GitHub Actions) plus automation tooling in Python and Bash, so that microservices ship to production with automated testing, image scanning and zero-downtime rollout. Apply Site Reliability Engineering practices end to end: define SLOs and error budgets, instrument services with Prometheus and Grafana, run blameless post-incident reviews, and introduce chaos-engineering experiments on non-production environments. Own infrastructure provisioning, version upgrades, scaling and cost optimisation across core AWS services — EC2, EKS, RDS, S3, IAM, VPC, ALB / NLB, KMS and Secrets Manager — with Ansible-driven configuration management for the remaining VM-based estate. Ensure security and regulatory compliance for a financial-services workload: IAM least-privilege, secrets management and rotation, encryption in transit and at rest, and full audit trails suitable for fintech change-control reviews. Collaborate with development teams on containerised microservices and hybrid / multi-cloud migrations (AWS primary, with Azure and GCP exposure), and mentor engineers on Terraform state management, Kubernetes security and observability best practices.
[...]
5-10 years of cloud / DevOps / SRE experience, with strong hands-on Kubernetes (EKS preferred) and AWS at production scale. Terraform expert — comfortable authoring reusable modules, managing remote state per environment, and reviewing other engineers' plans for cost and security impact. Solid automation scripting in Python and Bash, plus working Ansible for configuration management. Production CI/CD ownership in Jenkins and / or GitHub Actions, including GitOps delivery patterns. Practical SRE mindset: SLIs / SLOs, error budgets, monitoring with Prometheus / Grafana, on-call discipline. Exposure to multi-cloud (Azure / GCP) is a plus; financial-services or other regulated-environment experience is valued. CKA / CKAD and AWS certifications are a big plus but not mandatory. B.Tech / B.E. / MCA or equivalent experience.
[...]
FIS Global is one of the world's largest fintech companies, and its India engineering centres in Hyderabad, Bengaluru and Pune run cloud platforms that move enormous transaction volumes. This role sits at the intersection of Kubernetes, AWS and SRE for regulated financial workloads — rare, well-compensated experience that opens doors at any large bank, fintech or payments employer thereafter. Note the linked requisition is tagged as a Hong Kong shift; FIS also runs equivalent India-based Cloud Engineer / Senior Cloud Engineer openings, so search the FIS careers portal for the Hyderabad / Bengaluru / Pune variants too.
[...]
Apply via the official FIS careers portal (link below) and, in parallel, search the same portal for 'Cloud Engineer', 'Senior Cloud Engineer' and 'SRE' filtered to Hyderabad, Bengaluru and Pune — FIS posts near-identical India requisitions. Tailor your CV with concrete metrics, e.g. 'Managed 50+ node EKS clusters, cut deployment downtime by 70%, authored 20+ reusable Terraform modules'. Lead with EKS production scope and Terraform module ownership.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Senior Lead AI Engineer(MLX, Agentic AI, Gen AI platform Services) at Capital One | Jeremy
highlights:
Senior Lead AI Engineer(MLX, Agentic AI, Gen AI platform Services) at Capital One | Jeremy
[...]
# Senior Lead AI Engineer(MLX, Agentic AI, Gen AI platform Services)
[...]
Lead AI engineer role at Capital One's Intelligent Foundations and Experiences team, designing and optimizing large-scale LLM systems, foundation models, and agentic AI platforms for production banking applications.
[...]
At Capital One, we are creating responsible and reliable AI systems, changing banking for good. For years, Capital One has been an industry leader in using machine learning to create real-time, personalized customer experiences. Our investments in technology infrastructure and world-class talent — along with our deep experience in machine learning — position us to be at the forefront of enterprises leveraging AI. From informing customers about unusual charges to answering their questions in real time, our applications of AI & ML are bringing humanity and simplicity to banking. We are committed to continuing to build world-class applied science and engineering teams to deliver our industry leading capabilities with breakthrough product experiences and scalable, high-performance AI infrastructure. At Capital One, you will help bring the transformative power of emerging AI capabilities to reimagine how we serve our customers and businesses who have come to love the products and services we build.
[...]
The Intelligent Foundations and Experiences (IFX) team is at the center of bringing our vision for AI at Capital One to life. We work hand-in-hand with our partners across the company to advance the state of the art in science and AI engineering, and we build and deploy proprietary solutions that are central to our business and deliver value to millions of customers. Our AI models and platforms empower teams across Capital One to enhance their products with the transformative power of AI, in responsible and scalable ways for the highest leverage impact.
[...]

highlights:
# AI Scientist – Toronto, ON (Hybrid) | Generative AI, ML, Banking AI Role with Enterprise Impact
[...]
This AI Scientist role at CIBC (Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce) is a high-impact opportunity within the bank’s Advanced Analytics & AI division. The team focuses on building production-grade Machine Learning and Generative AI systems that power automation, document intelligence, fraud detection, and operational efficiency across enterprise banking. The ideal candidate is a STEM-trained professional with hands-on experience in Python, LLMs, and applied AI systems, capable of translating research concepts into scalable business solutions in a regulated financial environment.
[...]
This role is best suited for early-to-mid career AI practitioners who want to transition into enterprise AI engineering and applied research. You will collaborate with senior data scientists, ML engineers, and business stakeholders to deploy AI systems across real-world banking workflows.
[...]

[...]
: Mid-Level AI / Applied Machine Learning
[...]
This position is centered around building scalable AI systems inside a regulated banking ecosystem. Roughly 40% of the role focuses on building and optimizing Machine Learning models, including classification, prediction, and risk scoring systems. Another 30% is dedicated to Generative AI workflows, such as LLM orchestration, prompt engineering, and document understanding using OCR and NLP pipelines. Around 20% involves production engineering, including deploying models using Python, APIs (FastAPI/Flask), and cloud infrastructure (likely Azure). The remaining 10% is collaboration with business stakeholders across fraud, operations, and finance teams.

You will be expected to design AI automation pipelines that extract structured insights from unstructured documents, reduce manual processing overhead, and improve decision-making efficiency. A key expectation is translating research-level ML techniques into production-ready systems with reliability, scalability, and compliance in mind. Familiarity with frameworks like Scikit-learn, XGBoost, and modern LLM tooling will be critical for success.

results.title: Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People
highlights:
Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People

## Overview

JPMAM Data Science is dedicated to enhancing and streamlining every stage of the investment process, from financial analysis and portfolio management to client services and advisory. We analyse extensive collections of data including financial documents, analyst reports, news articles, meeting notes, and client communications to empower data-driven decision making and process automation. We are seeking passionate data scientists who can apply the latest methodologies to generate real-time and actionable insights.

The candidate must excel in working in a highly collaborative environment together with the business stakeholders, technologists, and control partners to deploy solutions into production. A strong passion for machine learning is essential, along with a commitment to continuous learning, research, and experimentation with new innovations in the field. Candidates should possess a solid understanding of modern NLP and/or financial knowledge, hands-on implementation experience, strong analytical thinking, and a keen interest in applying advanced analytics to solve complex problems in finance and asset management.

Job Responsibilities

Design and implement advanced techniques such as semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), named entity recognition (NER), prompt engineering, and personalization for content extraction, search, question answering, reasoning, and recommendation. Develop LLM, NLP, and ML solutions that address client requirements and drive business transformation. Work closely with partner teams—including Business, Technology, Product Management, Strategy, and Business Management—to deploy and scale developed models in production environments. Build comprehensive testing setups to evaluate model performance and ensure efficacy and reliability. Communicate results effectively to business stakeholders through written reports, visualizations, and presentations. Stay current with the latest research in LLM, ML, and data science, and proactively identify and leverage emerging techniques to drive ongoing enhancement.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills

Advanced degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or Machine LearningProven experience applying NLP, LLM, and ML techniques to solve high-impact business problems. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and familiarity with machine learning libraries and frameworks. Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong analytical skills with an understanding of financial markets and asset management.

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills.

results.title: Senior/Lead - Backend Engineer, Data Engineer - AI Engineering at FICO - United States Remote
highlights:
# Senior/Lead - Backend Engineer, Data Engineer - AI Engineering
[...]
FICO is a leading analytics and software company renowned for its FICO® Score, a tool widely used by lenders to assess credit risk. The company offers a comprehensive platform that leverages data, AI, and machine learning to power intelligent decision-making and customer engagement across various industries. FICO's solutions span fraud detection, credit scoring, and customer lifecycle management, making it vital to sectors such as finance and telecommunications. Its innovative products help businesses optimize outcomes through real-time analytics, business composability, and scenario management.
[...]
• Design and deploy scalable backend systems and data pipelines that seamlessly integrate applied AI capabilities into FICO's analytics and decision management platform. • Build high-quality solutions for data ingestion, transformation, and storage, ensuring reliable, high-throughput data flow for real-time analytics. • Design and implement LLM-powered solutions for decision automation, fraud investigation, and process automation within FICO's platform. • Develop sophisticated prompting strategies and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures tailored to high-stakes, mission-critical applications. • Partner with data scientists, ML engineers, and product teams to develop microservices and APIs that enable intelligent, data-driven decision-making. • Optimize distributed architectures and implement real-time processing frameworks to support high-volume, low-latency workloads. • Implement advanced monitoring, testing, and performance optimization techniques to ensure system reliability, security, and scalability. • Define and evolve architectural patterns that support FICO's analytics and decisioning solutions at scale. • Lead and mentor engineering team members, promoting best practices in software development, data engineering, AI integration, and systems design.
[...]
• 7+ years of experience in backend or data engineering, with a demonstrated track record of delivering complex, large-scale production systems. • Strong coding skills in Python, Go, Java, or equivalent languages, with a commitment to clean, maintainable, and well-tested code. • Hands-on experience with big data frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Kafka, Hadoop) and a strong understanding of both relational and NoSQL databases. • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and container orchestration tools such as Docker and Kubernetes. • Proven ability to design, build, and maintain scalable, production-grade backend systems with a focus on reliability and performance. • Solid experience with testing frameworks (including A/B testing), performance optimization, and production monitoring/observability practices. • Demonstrated experience integrating AI/ML solutions into production systems, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related techniques. • Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures and familiarity with vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector) is strongly preferred. • Strong problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to mentor engineers, influence technical direction, and collaborate effectively across disciplines. • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. An advanced degree is a plus but not required.

About The CompanyUSAA is a leading financial services organization dedicated to serving the unique needs of military members and their families. Our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service, and trusted advice. We strive to be the preferred choice within the military community by upholding our core values of honesty, integrity, loyalty, and service. USAA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment where our team members can grow and thrive. We also take pride in supporting active-duty military spouses, offering flexible work arrangements such as remote or hybrid options, in line with company policies and business needs. Our organization values innovation and continuous improvement, making us a dynamic and rewarding place to build your career.About The RoleWe are seeking a highly skilled Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to spearhead the strategic adoption and practical application of artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). This pivotal role involves identifying innovative AI solutions, whether emerging industry technologies, existing tools from other USAA divisions, or integrated platform solutions, to enhance productivity, automate processes, reduce operational costs, and unlock new revenue streams in the financial services sector. The successful candidate will actively scout and evaluate external AI tools and technologies through various channels, including professional networks, academic collaborations, industry conferences, and research publications, ensuring USAA remains at the forefront of technological advancements. The role emphasizes the development of intelligent work assistants that streamline routine tasks, thereby improving the member experience and operational efficiency while considering ethical implications and future potential of AI innovations.This position offers remote work flexibility within the continental U.S., with occasional travel requirements. Candidates residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office are expected to work on-site four days a week. results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Hirequorum
highlights:
Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Hirequorum
[...]
# Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II
[...]
We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS.
[...]
- End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach(prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy.
[...]
- Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference.
[...]
- Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation.
[...]
- Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails.
[...]
- Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration).
[...]
- MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies.
[...]
- Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs.
[...]
- Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs.
[...]
- Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments.
[...]
### Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities)
[...]
- 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems.
- MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
[...]
- Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals.
- Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents.
[...]
- Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA).
- Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services).
[...]
- Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale.
[...]
- Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses.
[...]
- Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems.
[...]
- Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM.
[...]
- Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana).
[...]
- Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral).
[...]
- Languages: Python, SQL
- Data: Snowflake, Postgres
- Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda)
- MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B
[...]
- Xsolis is an AI-driven technology company with a human-centered approach, fostering collaboration between healthcare providers and payers through real-time transparency, objective data for increased accuracy and alignment of medical necessity decisions, and more efficient outcomes. **What does a successful Technology Analyst- Data Engineer do?**

We’re looking for an early-career Data Engineer to help design, build, and support reliable data pipelines that move data from source systems into analytics and operational platforms. You’ll work closely with data engineers, analysts, and stakeholders to ensure data is accurate, timely, and scalable.
[...]
**What you will do:**
[...]
* Assist in designing and building data pipelines for data ingestion, transformation, and storage.
* Develop and maintain pipeline workflows to enable efficient data movement and processing.
* Support data modeling and schema design for analytics and operational use cases.
* Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve pipeline performance, reliability, and data quality.
[...]
**What you will need to have:**
[...]
* Recent graduate of a bachelor's degree completed in December 2024 or after in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Technical Project Management, or other Technology Degrees 
* 3.0+ GPA 
* 0-2 years of professional work experience 
**What would be great to have:**
[...]
* Proficiency in SQL and a solid understanding of relational databases.
* Basic knowledge of Python or Java (or similar scripting languages) for automation and data processing.
* Understanding of data warehousing concepts and familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure or similar).
* Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work with large datasets.
* Experience with Apache Spark, Hadoop, or similar big data frameworks.
* Familiarity with Databricks workflows or other workflow orchestration tools.
* Exposure to Databricks, Azure Data Factory, or GCP Dataflow.
* Knowledge of CI/CD practices and pipelines for data engineering.

results.title: Lead Data Scientist - Gen AI & Digital Twin - Caterpillar | Built In Chicago
highlights:
# Lead Data Scientist - Gen AI & Digital Twin
[...]
Lead development and integration of GPU-accelerated anomaly detection, digital twins, and GenAI-assisted predictive analytics for condition monitoring. Deploy and optimize models for NVIDIA GPUs and Jetson edge hardware, build RAG-enabled LLM diagnostic workflows, integrate multimodal telematics data, and collaborate with engineering teams for onboard simulation, testing, and hardware-software co-design.
[...]
Career Area:Technology, Digital and DataJob Description:Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc. When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do - but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here - we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.The Cat® Digital group is the digital and technology arm of Caterpillar Inc., responsible for bringing world class capabilities to our products and services. With over one million connected assets worldwide, we're focused on using data, advanced analytics, and AI capabilities to help our customers build a better world. To accomplish this, we're deploying analytics that generate insights, recommend optimized decisions, and improve products by intelligently integrating massive quantities of telematics information, transactional records, images, unstructured documents, and other data sources. Job Summary: The Condition Monitoring Analytics team of Cat Digital is seeking a Lead Data Scientist to be a technical expert, working in a team environment, to drive development & integration of digital twins & GenAI-assisted predictive analytics for condition monitoring of Caterpillar equipment.What You Will Do:Algorithm Development & Modeling
[...]
- Anomaly Detection: Design and implement GPU-accelerated machine learning models (e.g., XGBoost, autoencoders, and GANs using Tesseract) to identify fault patterns in timeseries sensor data.
[...]
- Digital Twin Engineering: Partner with engineering teams to develop onboard digital twins using NVIDIA architecture (e.g. PhysicsNeMo) to simulate, predict, and optimize the performance of heavy machinery
[...]
- Optimization: Profile and tune deep learning algorithms for maximum efficiency on NVIDIA GPU architectures, ensuring high throughput and low latency for real-time monitoring.
[...]
- Edge Deployment: Adapt and test algorithms for onboard architecture, leveraging tools like NVIDIA Jetson for ROM generation and real-time edge processing on Cat equipment.
[...]
- Hardware-Software Co-Design: Collaborate with hardware / simulation engineers to ensure algorithm compatibility with next-generation processors and specialized onboard compute modules.
[...]
- Simulation-Based Training: Use high-fidelity digital twins to simulate rare failure scenarios, ensuring the GenAI assistant provides accurate troubleshooting steps for edge-case mechanical issues.
[...]
- Automated Diagnostic Workflows: Develop Generative AI agents that synthesize telematics data to generate prioritized repairs for identified machine faults.
- Unified Data Orchestration: Integrate multi-modal outputs from condition monitoring analytics & asset life history to create a machine-specific context for AI assistant.
[...]
- Generative AI & LLMs: Proficiency in Fine-tuning and Prompt Engineering for Large Language Models, specifically using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
[...]
- Typically, a Bachelors, Masters, or PhD degree in Applied Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Predictive Analytics, Business Intelligence &
[...]
- Extensive experience with advanced data analysis, machine learning such as clustering, Log regressions, neural nets and statistical methods such as statistical process control, etc. (typically 8+ years)

The Corporate & Investment Bank is a global leader across investment banking, wholesale payments, markets and securities services. The world's most important corporations, governments and institutions entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. We provide strategic advice, raise capital, manage risk and extend liquidity in markets around the world. As an Applied AI ML Engineer-Vice President in our Corporate & Investment Bank, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying cutting-edge AI and machine learning solutions to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of our operations. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities for automation and process improvement, utilizing your expertise in machine learning and generative AI.Job Responsibilities

- Develop and implement machine learning models and algorithms to solve complex operational challenges.

- Design and deploy generative AI applications to automate and optimize business processes.

- Familiarity with MLOps practices, encompassing the full cycle from design, experimentation, deployment, to monitoring and maintenance of machine learning models.

[...]
- Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.
[...]
Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills

- Experience in the financial services industry, particularly within investment banking operations.
- Experience in developing AI solutions using agentic frameworks.
- Experience fine-tuning SLMs with approaches like LoRA, QLoRA and DoRA.
- Experience with prompt optimization frameworks such as AutoPrompt and DSPY to enhance the performance and effectiveness of prompt engineering.

results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
ll be doing:
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
Mentor team members, review
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
bonus if you:
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)
[...]
